page: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing a total of 640 GB of VRAM. The system is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 1360.0 GB of system memory, yielding 14.17 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates highly parallelized performance characteristics. While single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core, fall into the bottom 25%, multi-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core operations. Memory read bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 4205.12 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 193.62 GB/sec for large blocks. Redis SET operations also perform strongly, executing 61.66 million operations per second.

This server represents an extreme tier of resource density, trading off single-threaded CPU speed for massive parallel processing capabilities and memory capacity. Qualitatively, it is a specialized, high-cost hardware profile designed for workloads that can leverage its 16 GPUs and large memory footprint. It is best suited for large-scale deep learning training, massive parallel data science jobs, high-performance computing, and complex simulation workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for massive parallel workloads. It features 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 640 GB of total VRAM, paired with 96 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture and 1360.0 GB of system memory. While single-core CPU benchmarks are weak, the instance delivers strong multi-core performance and exceptional memory read bandwidth, reaching 4205.12 GB/sec for cached small blocks. Redis SET operations are also highly efficient, executing 61.66 million operations per second. With no local storage bundled, this high-density configuration trades off single-threaded speed for extreme parallel compute capacity. It is best suited for large-scale deep learning, data science, and high-performance computing workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 16 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 1360 GB of RAM, designed for massive parallel computing and deep learning.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g features 16 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 1360 GB RAM for parallel computing and deep learning.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform featuring sixteen dedicated GPUs and massive system memory for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 640 GB total VRAM
96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1360 GB of system memory
Strong multi-core CPU and memory read bandwidth performance
High-density parallel architecture with no bundled local storage
Designed for deep learning training and high-performance computing
